title: Parasites Regained

jokes.text: You stink, loser. Hey, Fry! Pizza going out!
jokes.setup: Generic insult followed by friendly greeting
jokes.punchline: Reveals the insult wasn't directed at Fry at all
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection joke - competent setup/payoff but not particularly memorable

jokes.text: Hello! Pizza delivery for... D. Frosted Wang?
jokes.setup: Standard pizza delivery greeting
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous fake name that sounds sexual
jokes.explanation: Classic juvenile humor - gets a laugh but relies on easy sexual innuendo

jokes.text: One time was funny. But every week for ten years? Not as funny. True, not as funny. But still somewhat funny.
jokes.setup: Fry complaining about repetitive prank calls
jokes.punchline: Other character's grudging admission it's still somewhat funny
jokes.explanation: Nice meta-commentary on running gags - earns its laughs through honest observation

jokes.text: There'll be no promotions unless somebody dies. And even then, only if we can't bring them back as a zombie, like Scruffy.
jokes.setup: Fry asking about promotions
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of zombie resurrection policy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Futurama sci-fi absurdism - treats the impossible as mundane bureaucracy

jokes.text: Life and death are a seamless continuum. Mm-hmm.
jokes.setup: Being referenced as a zombie
jokes.punchline: Scruffy's philosophical acceptance of his undead state
jokes.explanation: Classic Scruffy - perfectly in character with his zen janitor persona

jokes.text: I could use a shot in the arm. I get shot in the leg!
jokes.setup: Fry asking for encouragement ('shot in the arm')
jokes.punchline: Roberto literally shoots him in the leg
jokes.explanation: Good literal interpretation of idiom - enhanced by Roberto's violent character

jokes.text: What? A fella can't drop in on old friends and hold them hostage?
jokes.setup: Fry asking why Roberto is there
jokes.punchline: Roberto treating hostage-taking as casual social visit
jokes.explanation: Roberto's psychotic politeness is perfectly in character and absurd

jokes.text: Listen up, piggies, I want a hovercopter, and an unmarked sandwich, and a new face with like a, a Hugh Grant look.
jokes.setup: Standard hostage demands
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous requests ending with Hugh Grant face
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from practical to absurd - Hugh Grant reference is dated but specific

jokes.text: He's bluffing. Ouch! He's not bluffing!
jokes.setup: Character doubts Roberto's threat
jokes.punchline: Immediate painful proof that Roberto wasn't bluffing
jokes.explanation: Classic setup/payoff structure - predictable but well-executed

jokes.text: You call for a chopper? Nah, I specifically said hovercopter!
jokes.setup: Police bring helicopter as requested
jokes.punchline: Roberto pedantically corrects to his sci-fi request
jokes.explanation: Good sci-fi world-building humor - Roberto being picky about future tech

jokes.text: You know, URL, sometimes it's almost too much for me. You mean the respect we get? And the high level of job satisfaction? There's that, but I'm also talking about the non-stop promotions and the free hot rod.
jokes.setup: Smitty complaining about police work being 'too much'
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's actually bragging about all the perks
jokes.explanation: Mild misdirection - setup implies complaint but reveals humble bragging

jokes.text: Mm. I do love a man in uniform. Ahem! I mean a uniform that doesn't involve short pants.
jokes.setup: Amy expressing attraction to men in uniform
jokes.punchline: Awkward clarification excluding Fry's delivery boy shorts
jokes.explanation: Standard awkward save joke - works but not particularly creative

jokes.text: I can see I have no future here. My only option is to resign with dignity. Here are my shorts.
jokes.setup: Fry declaring dignified resignation
jokes.punchline: Visual of him removing his uniform shorts, presumably standing pantless
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy - dignity undermined by practical necessity of removing uniform

jokes.text: You think you can just waltz in here with no pants and become a cop? That's the plan.
jokes.setup: Officer questioning Fry's pantless police application
jokes.punchline: Fry's matter-of-fact confirmation of absurd plan
jokes.explanation: Fry's blunt honesty about his ridiculous situation is endearing

jokes.text: I like you, kid. I got no pants on, either. I can see that. You're quite a bit taller than me.
jokes.setup: Officer bonding with Fry over shared pantlessness
jokes.punchline: Fry's awkward observation about height difference (implying visible anatomy)
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable visual comedy - pushes boundaries while staying character-appropriate

jokes.text: Weep, wop, beep! Wow. That would be impressive if you were a human. Yeah.
jokes.setup: Robot demonstrating sound effects capability
jokes.punchline: Fry's backhanded compliment about it being unimpressive for a robot
jokes.explanation: Decent deflation of robot's pride - competent but not exceptional

jokes.text: I'll make this short and sweet, 'cause I'm PMSing like a lumberjack.
jokes.setup: Chief beginning academy speech
jokes.punchline: Unexpected feminine vulnerability combined with masculine metaphor
jokes.explanation: Great character establishment - tough female chief with crude honesty

jokes.text: You're gonna get your boob scuffed. But if you've got the vulva to stick it out, I'll be proud to call you ladies policemen.
jokes.setup: Chief giving motivational speech about police academy challenges
jokes.punchline: Gender-flipped versions of masculine clichés (balls/guts becomes vulva)
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that subverts masculine police academy tropes - very quotable

jokes.text: Now, can you actually turn on the ignition? I don't have any hands.
jokes.setup: Driving instructor asking robot to start car
jokes.punchline: Robot's practical limitation revealed after the fact
jokes.explanation: Good reveal of design flaw - timing works well for the deflation

jokes.text: Well, it's that time of the month. Graduation day. So grow a pair and put them together for our new officers!
jokes.setup: 'Time of the month' implies menstruation reference
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's graduation day, then subverts 'grow a pair' into applauding
jokes.explanation: Nice double misdirection - both setup lines pay off differently than expected

jokes.text: Hooray! Free sky sticks! Oh! Police brutality!
jokes.setup: Citizens excited about free batons
jokes.punchline: Immediate cry of police brutality when they're used
jokes.explanation: Good ironic reversal - citizens get exactly what they asked for

jokes.text: And Smitty was just a few days from retirement. Wh-What happened? He took a early retirement. Damn.
jokes.setup: Classic 'few days from retirement' suggests death
jokes.punchline: Anticlimatic reveal he just retired early
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of cop show trope - anticlimactic in a funny way

jokes.text: Oh, I just said that for Fry's benefit. Made the poor fellow feel better about his pointless job.
jokes.setup: Professor's caring 'good news' catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was condescending pity for Fry's meaningless existence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor callousness - retroactively ruins his seemingly kind catchphrase

jokes.text: I've improved on Fry by sticking some wheels on a board
jokes.setup: Professor claiming to have improved on Fry
jokes.punchline: His improvement is literally just a skateboard
jokes.explanation: Great Professor moment - his 'improvement' on human employee is absurdly basic

jokes.text: That dangerous 3D planet? Can't we just send our avatars? No, it's cheaper just to have you die.
jokes.setup: Bender suggesting safer Avatar-style approach to dangerous planet
jokes.punchline: Professor's casual acceptance of employee death as cost-saving measure
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - Professor's capitalism taken to logical extreme

jokes.text: Whoa. 15 miles over the speed of light. That's a violation of the law of Lorentz's invariants, baby.
jokes.setup: Traffic stop scenario
jokes.punchline: Scientific law treated as traffic law with URL's signature 'baby'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant sci-fi humor - physics violation as traffic ticket, perfectly delivered by URL

jokes.text: An electric wall, eh? I can't see the harm in peeing on that.
jokes.setup: Fry encountering electric fence
jokes.punchline: His spectacularly poor decision-making on full display
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry stupidity - audience knows exactly what will happen but it's still funny

jokes.text: Whoa! Where'd my man learn that? Sunny D commercial.
jokes.setup: URL impressed by Fry's apparent martial arts skill
jokes.punchline: Fry learned it from a beverage commercial
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist deflation - impressive skill has ridiculous mundane source

jokes.text: Erwin Schrodinger, huh? What's in the box, Schrodinger? Um... a cat, some poison, and a cesium atom.
jokes.setup: Police checkpoint questioning famous physicist
jokes.punchline: Schrodinger literally has the contents of his famous thought experiment
jokes.explanation: Clever science humor - makes abstract thought experiment concrete and suspicious

jokes.text: Is it alive or dead? Alive or dead? Answer him, fool. It's a superposition of both states until you open it and collapse the wave function. Says you.
jokes.setup: Police interrogation about cat's status
jokes.punchline: Schrodinger gives actual quantum physics explanation, dismissed casually
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to setup - treats complex physics as evasive criminal behavior

jokes.text: There's also a lot of drugs in there.
jokes.setup: Complex physics explanation about box contents
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of actual criminal activity
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - after all the physics talk, actual drugs are anticlimactic yet incriminating

jokes.text: Cat's gonna testify. Guys like this really bust my uterus.
jokes.setup: Standard police procedure discussion
jokes.punchline: Cat as witness plus Chief's feminine twist on masculine phrase
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Chief's speech pattern plus absurd legal procedure

jokes.text: Ever heard of the Future Crimes Division? Tomorrow, I have.
jokes.setup: Chief asking about Future Crimes Division
jokes.punchline: URL's literal interpretation as time travel
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay - URL treats 'heard of' as literally hearing in the future

jokes.text: Well, I guess you should have had two eyes.
jokes.setup: Leela presumably having trouble seeing on alien planet
jokes.punchline: Bender's cruel comment about her one eye
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender cruelty - perfectly in character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Screw that! I ain't no delivery boy. I'm the company chef. Oh, you're the chef, are you? Then why don't you make us something nice to eat? Here's your damn peanut butter and jelly sandwich!
jokes.setup: Bender refusing delivery work, claiming to be chef
jokes.punchline: His 'chef' skills produce basic PB&J sandwich
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Bender's pretensions - his cooking is as lazy as expected

jokes.text: That's ridiculous. Only horoscopes can predict the future.
jokes.setup: Discussion of predicting future crimes scientifically
jokes.punchline: Fry dismisses science in favor of astrology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry logic - rejects sophisticated technology for pseudoscience

jokes.text: We call him... Pickles. On account of it's like he's floating in a jar? Exactly.
jokes.setup: Dramatic introduction of sophisticated crime-predicting oracle
jokes.punchline: Mundane name with literal visual explanation
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of dramatic buildup - sophisticated tech gets pedestrian nickname

jokes.text: What's pink polka-dots? Clown slaughter. It happens more often than you'd think.
jokes.setup: Explanation of crime prediction color coding
jokes.punchline: Unexpectedly specific and disturbing crime category presented casually
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - specific absurd crime treated as mundane statistical reality

jokes.text: My kajigger! Hattie McDoogal.
jokes.setup: Standard murder scene dialogue
jokes.punchline: Victim's name is absurdly old-fashioned and silly
jokes.explanation: Weak humor - just relies on silly name without much craft or character work

jokes.text: Uh-oh, we only have a half hour to chill out before we head over.
jokes.setup: Urgent time pressure to prevent murder
jokes.punchline: URL's casual approach to time management
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - URL's laid-back attitude toward life-or-death situations

jokes.text: He was going to murder you, marry your parakeets, and then poison them with an arsenic-laced cuttlebone. Classic move.
jokes.setup: Explanation of murder plot
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd inheritance scheme treated as routine criminal behavior
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating absurdity - each plot point gets more ridiculous, 'classic move' is perfect

jokes.text: Now, if you'll excuse me, I got to go drop a big one. Eight pounds. I'm naming her Burt. Mazel tov, Chief. That's a beautiful bathroom baby.
jokes.setup: Chief's crude euphemism for using bathroom
jokes.punchline: Reveals she's actually giving birth, Fry's awkward congratulations
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - crude setup becomes touching moment, Fry's response is perfectly awkward

jokes.text: The thief seems to be some sort of ghost. Or is he?
jokes.setup: Fry dramatically questioning invisible thief's nature
jokes.punchline: His serious detective work on obviously ridiculous conclusion
jokes.explanation: Mild character humor - Fry taking detective work too seriously with obvious answer

jokes.text: Things used to be funny around here. Noses were picked, pants fell down. So what changed? Only Fry could say, and he's gone.
jokes.setup: Characters noting lack of humor in workplace
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary on Fry being the source of comedy in the show
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-humor about the show's own comedy dynamics - earns its self-reference

jokes.text: We had nothing to say to each other, so we had to listen to Bender's Eagles album for 27 hours. Beats talking to you, witchy woman.
jokes.setup: Leela complaining about boring trip with Bender
jokes.punchline: Bender's insult using Eagles song reference
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction - Bender's Eagles obsession pays off in insult

jokes.text: Look at you. Hubba hubba. Your pants go almost all the way to your ankles.
jokes.setup: Amy admires Fry's police uniform
jokes.punchline: Impressed that his pants actually reach near his ankles for once
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Fry's previous uniform, Amy's shallow focus on appearance improvement

jokes.text: Normally, when a cop asks me that, I spray whip cream in his eyes and run away, but to ditch these losers, gladly.
jokes.setup: Police officer asking Bender to leave
jokes.punchline: Bender's specific criminal escape method and eagerness to leave friends
jokes.explanation: Good Bender moment - specific criminal behavior plus casual cruelty to friends

jokes.text: Normally, when a cop asks me that, I spray whip cream in his eyes and run away, but to ditch these losers, gladly.
jokes.setup: Fry asks Bender to leave the building
jokes.punchline: Bender reveals his usual anti-cop strategy but cooperates to escape coworkers
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - both anti-authority and willing to use cops to escape social obligations

jokes.text: I may have a burgling familiarity with it.
jokes.setup: Fry asking if Bender knows the location
jokes.punchline: Bender's euphemistic way of admitting criminal knowledge
jokes.explanation: Nice Bender wordplay - sophisticated phrasing for criminal admission

jokes.text: The Maltese Liquor-- the strongest, the most valuable malt liquor ever brewed! And soon it will be mine. I'm sorry. You were saying?
jokes.setup: Bender getting excited about theft opportunity
jokes.punchline: His fake innocent question after revealing criminal intent
jokes.explanation: Good Bender moment - can't help revealing his greed then tries to play innocent

jokes.text: You wrote 'commit the crime'!
jokes.setup: Bender claiming he wrote down 'avoid committing the crime'
jokes.punchline: Visual revelation that he actually wrote the opposite
jokes.explanation: Good visual payoff - Bender's predictable dishonesty revealed through his notes

jokes.text: That's me! I recognize the face. You shot me! You miserable dingus!
jokes.setup: Fry sees prediction of himself shooting Bender
jokes.punchline: Bender's immediate angry reaction to future betrayal
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Bender treating future event as current betrayal

jokes.text: No! No! What have I will have done? Man, that's heavy. Osmium heavy.
jokes.setup: Fry's time-travel grammar confusion after shooting Bender
jokes.punchline: URL's response escalates from slang to scientific reference
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay - takes 'heavy' from emotional to literal scientific meaning

jokes.text: You... and the chief? Mm-hmm. We been gettin' it on. Right there in your chair. I'm like, 'Oh, baby! Oh, baby!' And she's like, 'Yeah. Yeah. Yeah.'
jokes.setup: Fry discovering URL and Chief's relationship
jokes.punchline: URL's graphic oversharing about their intimate moments
jokes.explanation: Standard TMI humor - gets cringe laughs but not particularly crafted

jokes.text: I would never shoot Bender. Never! He taught me how to shave.
jokes.setup: Fry declares loyalty to Bender
jokes.punchline: Touching but absurd reason involving basic hygiene instruction
jokes.explanation: Sweet but ridiculous basis for friendship loyalty - very Fry

jokes.text: I could never shoot you. Never! You taught me how to shave. See? Just get out of here. Go!
jokes.setup: Fry's emotional declaration about not shooting Bender
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific reason (shaving lessons) for their friendship
jokes.explanation: Nice callback - touching friendship moment based on mundane life skill

jokes.text: The Maltese Liquor done destroyed their brains. Fools should have known it's fatal for anyone but robots and Billy Dee Williams.
jokes.setup: Explanation of deadly alcohol affecting friends
jokes.punchline: Specific exception for random celebrity alongside robots
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurd specificity - Billy Dee Williams as random exception is perfectly random

jokes.text: Fate is a freaky mistress. Much like the chief.
jokes.setup: Philosophical observation about fate
jokes.punchline: URL comparing fate to his sexual partner
jokes.explanation: Competent character moment - URL brings everything back to his relationship

jokes.text: In order to fix your leaky roof, I'll need to spend two or three hours down here in the wine cellar. I'll be upstairs putting batteries in things.
jokes.setup: Bender's obviously fake excuse to case the wine cellar
jokes.punchline: Homeowner's equally suspicious activity excuse
jokes.explanation: Good setup for heist - both characters clearly have ulterior motives

jokes.text: Invisible safe, eh?
jokes.setup: Bender discovers invisible safe
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact acceptance of futuristic security device
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag setup - Bender's professional thief reaction to security measures

jokes.text: Either you're going to shoot me, or I'm going to spray whip cream in your eyes and walk out of here like a big shot.
jokes.setup: Standoff between Fry and Bender
jokes.punchline: Bender threatens to use Roberto's whip cream escape tactic
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier episode, showing characters learning from each other

jokes.text: Either you're going to shoot me, or I'm going to spray whip cream in your eyes and walk out of here like a big shot.
jokes.setup: Standoff between Fry and Bender
jokes.punchline: Bender references his signature escape method from earlier
jokes.explanation: Callback to Bender's established criminal method, maintaining character consistency

jokes.text: Do you have any idea what a burden it is to know everything that will ever happen? To never be surprised. To know the punch line of every joke hours in advance. Like watching Leno.
jokes.setup: Pickles explaining burden of omniscience
jokes.punchline: Compares it to predictable late-night TV
jokes.explanation: Decent comparison - philosophical burden compared to mundane TV experience

jokes.text: Funny thing about destiny. Sometimes fate has other plans.
jokes.setup: Pickles insisting Fry must follow destiny
jokes.punchline: Fry's pseudo-philosophical rejection with confused logic
jokes.explanation: Good Fry moment - tries to sound wise but logic makes no sense

jokes.text: Oh, nuts. You shot me! You miserable dingus! And now the part I didn't show you.
jokes.setup: Prediction comes true with Fry shooting Pickles instead of Bender
jokes.punchline: Reveals this was hidden part of prediction all along
jokes.explanation: Good plot twist - the misdirection was part of the plan all along

jokes.text: Finally, blissful ignorance. I have no idea what's going to happen next. Did you get that, Chief? Huh?
jokes.setup: Pickles celebrates not knowing the future anymore
jokes.punchline: Immediately asks about recording, showing he still doesn't grasp his situation
jokes.explanation: Good irony - celebrating ignorance while missing obvious trap around him

jokes.text: But how could you know my prediction was fake? I knew something was wrong, but I couldn't put my finger on it. Then it hit me. You showed Bender sharing the deadly booze with our friends. Bender would never share. The very idea!
jokes.setup: Pickles asks how they knew prediction was fake
jokes.punchline: Fry identifies impossibility - Bender sharing anything
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-based detection - using Bender's selfishness as key evidence

jokes.text: So we set up this little charade based on an episode of Speed Buggy.
jokes.setup: Explaining how they outsmarted the Oracle
jokes.punchline: Credits obscure cartoon as inspiration for police work
jokes.explanation: Good callback - ridiculous cartoon becomes actual police procedure inspiration

jokes.text: I hear by promotion, executive delivery boy. Executive? It's a meaning less title. But it helps insecure people feel better about themselves. I feel better about myself!
jokes.setup: Professor giving Fry a promotion to Executive Delivery Boy
jokes.punchline: Admits it's meaningless but Fry is immediately satisfied
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending - Professor's cynical manipulation works exactly as intended on naive Fry

jokes.text: Executive? It's a meaningless title. But it helps insecure people feel better about themselves. I feel better about myself!
jokes.setup: Professor explains the promotion is meaningless
jokes.punchline: Fry immediately proves the Professor's point about insecure people
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending - Fry immediately confirming Professor's cynical psychology about meaningless titles

tmdb_overview: After Nibbler falls ill, the crew shrinks down for a dangerous mission into a desert world contained within his litter box.
